## Summary
The baryon acoustic peak at ~150 Mpc comoving separation in the galaxy correlation function serves as a cosmic standard ruler. This pattern, imprinted at recombination and preserved in large-scale structure, enables precise distance measurements across redshift.

## Evidence
First detected in SDSS LRG data at z = 0.35, now measured by BOSS, eBOSS, and DESI across 0.1 < z < 4.2. The peak position is robust to nonlinear evolution, with reconstruction techniques reducing degradation to sub-percent levels. BAO distances constrain H(z)rs and DA(z)/rs to 0.5–2% precision.

## Significance
BAO is the cleanest large-scale structure probe, with systematics well below statistical errors for current surveys. It anchors the cosmic distance ladder at intermediate redshifts and is central to all modern dark energy constraints.
